findings.title: Overview balances mission, domain, and delegation model
findings.detail: Pre-pass reported seven overview lines and zero waste-pattern hits. The Overview states what Gary does, physician/med-ed audience framing, Marcus delegation, gamma-api-mastery execution, woodshed/debug paths, style-bible refresh policy, and memory learning — load-bearing for judgment, not redundant with Identity alone.

findings.title: Communication Style uses concrete agent-to-agent examples
findings.detail: Bullet examples (parameter strings, visual reasoning, self-assessment scores) are intentional persona investment per agent-specific guidance; they reduce generic output without repeating the same idea elsewhere verbatim.

findings.title: Capabilities table routes depth to references cleanly
findings.detail: SKILL.md stays ~109 lines with procedural activation and a capability matrix pointing to references/*.md — matches multi-capability progressive disclosure; no large inline schemas or tables that should be extracted.

findings.title: Doc refresh and exemplar workflow mix procedure with clear Gary/evaluator split
findings.detail: Mandatory doc refresh before woodshed addresses API drift; integration section states Gary supplies analytical judgment while GammaEvaluator handles execution — appropriate intelligence placement for markdown prompts.

findings.title: Pre-pass flagged no formal progression markers on SKILL.md
findings.detail: prompt-metrics-prepass reported has_progression: false. On Activation still branches Headless, Interactive, and Woodshed with distinct load steps, so behavior is not under-specified; the gap is mainly metadata/convention alignment if downstream tooling expects explicit progression blocks.
findings.action: If BMAD or report pipelines require progression fields, add concise explicit gates (e.g., when to load which reference after envelope parse) without duplicating reference bodies.

findings.title: Reference prompts assume SKILL.md persona and config context
findings.detail: There are zero root-level capability .md files (pre-pass total_prompts: 0). Reference files omit YAML config headers with {communication_language}; under heavy context compaction, an executing model might lack language variables unless SKILL.md remains loaded. This is a mild risk, not a broken self-reference pattern — no back-references were detected in pre-pass.
findings.action: Optional: add a one-line session anchor (identity + language from config) to the highest-traffic references, or introduce thin root stubs that repeat config header and link to references.

skillmd_assessment.notes: 109 lines, ~2.5k tokens estimated; Overview is within sentence-count guidance; Principles and Does Not Do add constraints without philosophical bloat. Inline data extraction not needed.

summary.assessment: Prompt craft is strong: pre-pass showed no defensive-padding, back-reference, or wall-of-text hits; SKILL.md carries persona and routing while references hold capability depth. No critical craft defects; minor gaps are tooling-oriented progression metadata and optional compaction hardening for reference-only capabilities.
summary.top_improvement: If orchestration or scanners expect root-level capability prompts with config headers, add thin wrappers; otherwise keep current progressive disclosure and only add explicit progression metadata if required.
